name: The Mystery House
description: Rain lashes against broken windows as thunder splits the night sky. You stand before Blackwood Manor, a Victorian mansion long abandoned by the living — but not, the locals say, by the dead. The iron gates hang open like broken teeth. Dead vines choke the crumbling facade. Lightning illuminates windows that seem to watch you with malevolent intent. Your car died a mile back. The storm is worsening. The only shelter is within those rotting walls. As you step through the threshold, the front door slams shut behind you. The lock clicks. There is no going back.

entrance-hall.name: Grand Entrance Hall
entrance-hall.description: A vast entry hall stretches before you, once magnificent, now draped in decay. A crystal chandelier hangs by a single chain, swaying slightly though there is no breeze. The marble floor is cracked and stained with what might be old blood — or just rust. Twin staircases curve upward into shadow. Portraits line the walls, their painted eyes following your movement. The air is thick with the smell of mold and something sweeter — like lilies at a funeral. A grand corridor leads north deeper into the house. To the east, a door stands slightly ajar, revealing a dim library. West leads to what was once a parlor.

hazards.description: The chandelier above sways dangerously, its chain creaking with rust.
hazards.deathText: With a sound like screaming metal, the chandelier chain snaps. Crystal and iron crash down. Your final thought is how beautiful the shattered light looks spreading across the marble.

library.name: The Library
library.description: Walls of books reach toward a vaulted ceiling lost in shadow. Most volumes are rotted beyond reading, but some remain intact — forbidden grimoires, journals written in trembling hands, medical texts from when surgery was butchery. A massive oak desk dominates the center, its surface covered in scattered papers and dried ink. The windows are boarded shut, but moonlight seeps through cracks, painting silver stripes across the dusty floor. A leather armchair faces the cold fireplace, positioned as if someone just stood up. You can feel eyes watching from the shadows between shelves. The entrance hall is west. A narrow door to the north is marked with strange symbols.

hazards.description: The books seem to whisper when you're not looking directly at them — voices layered in forgotten languages.
hazards.deathText: The whispers crescendo into screams. Books fly from shelves like ravens. Pages slice skin like paper knives. The library devours you, adding your screams to its eternal collection.

parlor.name: The Parlor
parlor.description: A once-elegant sitting room now home to rot and shadow. Moth-eaten curtains hang in tatters. A grand piano stands in the corner, keys yellowed and cracked — yet sometimes, when the wind moans through broken panes, you swear you hear a chord. Victorian furniture is arranged as if expecting guests who never arrived. Or perhaps they did arrive, and never left. A music box sits on the mantle, its mechanism exposed and frozen. Photographs in silver frames show the Blackwood family — mother, father, three children — their faces scratched out by frantic fingers. The entrance hall is east. A door to the south is locked with a silver padlock.

hazards.description: The piano keys occasionally press down on their own, playing discordant notes that set your teeth on edge.
hazards.deathText: The piano erupts into a frenzied symphony, keys slamming with impossible force. The sound bores into your skull like an auger. Your ears bleed. Your mind shatters. You collapse, and the music plays on.

dining-room.name: The Dining Room
dining-room.description: A long table dominates the room, set for thirteen guests. China plates are crusted with ancient food that never decayed — just fossilized where it sat. Crystal wine glasses still contain dark liquid. Candelabras hold candles burned down to nubs of wax. The wallpaper depicts a hunting scene, but the painted figures seem to move in the corner of your vision. A grandfather clock in the corner is stopped at midnight, its pendulum hanging still. The walls are stained with handprints — small ones, child-sized, leading up to the ceiling. The entrance hall is south. Double doors to the north lead to the kitchen. An archway east opens into a conservatory.

hazards.description: The temperature drops suddenly, your breath misting in the air. The candles flicker to life on their own.
hazards.deathText: The ghost of the thirteenth guest manifests, a woman in Victorian dress with hollow eyes and a slit throat. She beckons you to sit. You cannot refuse. The wine is poison. You drink anyway.

kitchen.name: The Kitchen
kitchen.description: A cavernous industrial kitchen from a bygone era. Copper pots hang from hooks, green with verdigris. A massive cast-iron stove hunches against the wall like a sleeping beast. The butcher's block is scarred with cuts that go too deep, stained too dark. Knives are embedded in the walls at odd angles — thrown in panic or rage. The pantry door hangs open, revealing empty shelves and scratch marks on the inside. A meat locker to the west is sealed with a heavy bolt. The dining room is south. A cellar door to the east is secured with a heavy padlock.

hazards.description: Something drips from the ceiling — too dark and thick to be water. It smells of copper and decay.
hazards.deathText: The dripping becomes a deluge. Rotted blood cascades from the ceiling, filling the room with nauseating speed. You slip, fall, drown in the sins of the house. Your body is never found.

conservatory.name: The Conservatory
conservatory.description: A glass-walled room that should be filled with light but somehow remains dark. Dead plants fill raised beds — roses that grew into thorny masses before dying, ivy that strangled itself, a small tree twisted into an agonized shape. The glass is clouded with decades of grime, but occasionally lightning reveals the overgrown garden beyond. A fountain in the center is dry, its basin cracked. The statue that once crowned it — a weeping angel — is missing its head. Muddy footprints circle the fountain, too small for adult feet. The dining room is west. A greenhouse passage leads south.

meat-locker.name: The Meat Locker
meat-locker.description: A walk-in freezer that hasn't been cold for decades. Rusted hooks hang from the ceiling like waiting gallows. The walls are stained with things that shouldn't be in a meat locker — handprints, messages scratched into the metal: 'IT WON'T LET US LEAVE' and 'THE CHILDREN ARE STILL HUNGRY.' Something large is wrapped in canvas in the corner, human-shaped. You don't want to look closer. The door can only be opened from the outside — getting trapped here would be death. The kitchen is east.

hazards.description: The door swings slightly on its hinges, threatening to close and trap you inside.
hazards.deathText: The door slams shut. The bolt slides home with a final click. You hammer on the steel until your hands are bloody. No one hears. No one ever hears. Your frozen corpse will wait decades for the next curious soul.

hidden-study.name: The Hidden Study
hidden-study.description: A secret room behind the library, barely larger than a closet. The walls are covered in occult symbols drawn in what looks like ash — or maybe burned bone. A small altar bears a tarnished silver bowl and ritual implements. Books of black magic fill a shelf, their pages filled with madness. A journal lies open on a lectern, its final entry dated the night the Blackwood family disappeared: 'The ritual is complete. We are eternal now. We are never alone now. God forgive us for what we've done to the children.' A pentagram is burned into the wooden floor. The library is south. A concealed passage in the floor leads down.

hazards.description: The symbols on the walls pulse with a sickly light, and you hear children singing nursery rhymes backwards.
hazards.deathText: The pentagram erupts with black flame. Shadows coalesce into forms — the Blackwood children, their faces twisted, their eyes empty. They were made eternal through agony. They want you to join them. The ritual takes only moments. The screaming takes forever.

greenhouse.name: The Greenhouse
greenhouse.description: A humid glass structure attached to the house, somehow warmer than it should be. Plants grow here still — wrong plants, with leaves that move like fingers and flowers that smell of rotting meat. Venus flytraps have grown to monstrous size. Thorned vines creep across the floor like serpents. In the center is a willow tree that shouldn't be able to grow indoors, its drooping branches forming a curtain around something buried beneath its roots. The conservatory is north. You sense something important is here, something that binds the house's dark power.

hazards.description: The vines shift and writhe, slowly encircling your ankles like patient predators.
hazards.deathText: The carnivorous plants strike as one. Vines wrap around your limbs, pulling you down. Thorns pierce skin. The flytraps close over your face. The garden feeds. It's always hungry.

cellar.name: The Wine Cellar
cellar.description: Stone steps descend into a vast cellar carved from living rock. Wine racks stretch into darkness, bottles thick with dust and cobwebs. Most are empty now, broken in some long-ago rampage. The floor is sticky with ancient spills — or so you tell yourself. Chains hang from the walls for no purpose you can imagine. A wooden coffin rests against the far wall, lid slightly ajar. Something is carved into the stone ceiling: 'REST ETERNAL, BARTHOLOMEW BLACKWOOD, WHO DARED DEFY DEATH AND DAMNED HIS LINE.' An iron door to the west is sealed with arcane symbols — the final seal. The kitchen is up a flight of stairs to the west, but the cellar door is locked from this side.

hazards.description: A wet, raspy breathing echoes from the coffin. Something inside is dreaming, and dreams are stirring.
hazards.deathText: Bartholomew Blackwood rises from his coffin, patriarch of the damned, the warlock who traded his family's souls for immortality. His touch is ice. His eyes are flames. He drinks deep. Your body joins the others in the darkness, one more servant in his eternal court.

brass-candlestick.name: Brass Candlestick
brass-candlestick.description: A heavy brass candlestick, tarnished with age but still solid. It could serve as a weapon — or perhaps light the way, if you had a candle.
brass-candlestick.pickupText: You take the candlestick from the entrance hall table. It's reassuringly heavy in your hand.
brass-candlestick.roomText: A brass candlestick sits on a side table near the door, one of a pair — the other is missing.

ancient-journal.name: Ancient Journal
ancient-journal.description: A leather-bound journal filled with frantic handwriting. It belonged to Lady Margaret Blackwood. The entries detail her husband's descent into obsession with forbidden knowledge, the rituals in the hidden study, the night they sacrificed something precious to gain eternal life. The final entry: 'The children are crying. They won't stop crying. They don't understand what we've made them.'
ancient-journal.pickupText: You take the journal from the desk. The leather is disturbingly warm, as if recently held.
ancient-journal.roomText: A leather journal lies open on the oak desk, its pages covered in desperate handwriting.

silver-key.name: Silver Key
silver-key.description: A small silver key, ornately carved with roses. It's labeled in faded ink: 'Parlor.' This would unlock the door from the parlor to the greenhouse passage.
silver-key.pickupText: You take the silver key from a desk drawer. It's ice-cold against your palm.
silver-key.roomText: A small silver key gleams in the half-open drawer of the desk.

music-box.name: Music Box
music-box.description: A delicate music box with a ballerina frozen mid-pirouette. The mechanism is rusted, but if wound, it might still play. The tune is a lullaby — the kind you sing to children to keep nightmares away. How ironic.
music-box.pickupText: You take the music box. The ballerina's painted face seems sad, as if she knows something you don't.
music-box.roomText: A music box sits on the mantle, its mechanisms exposed and frozen.

family-photo.name: Family Photograph
family-photo.description: A silver-framed photograph of the Blackwood family: Lord Bartholomew with his dark beard, Lady Margaret in Victorian lace, and three children — two boys and a girl, none older than ten. All their faces have been violently scratched out, except for one child whose face remains intact, staring at you with knowing eyes.
family-photo.pickupText: You take the photograph. The intact child's eyes seem to follow you, pleading silently.
family-photo.roomText: A silver-framed photograph rests on a side table, its glass cracked but intact.

rusted-cleaver.name: Rusted Cleaver
rusted-cleaver.description: A butcher's cleaver, blade stained with rust and other things. The handle is carved with initials: 'B.B.' — Bartholomew Blackwood. Whatever this was used for, it wasn't just cutting meat.
rusted-cleaver.pickupText: You pull the cleaver from the butcher's block. The blade whispers as it comes free.
rusted-cleaver.roomText: A rusted cleaver is embedded deep in the butcher's block, as if left there mid-swing.

brass-key.name: Brass Key
brass-key.description: A heavy brass key labeled 'Wine Cellar.' It would open the padlocked door in the kitchen that leads down to the cellars — the deepest, darkest part of the house.
brass-key.pickupText: You take the brass key from its hook. Something about holding it fills you with dread.
brass-key.roomText: A brass key hangs on a hook beside the pantry, labeled in faded script.

withered-rose.name: Withered Rose
withered-rose.description: A single black rose, perfectly preserved though long dead. It exudes a faint sweet scent — like perfume mixed with decay. The petals feel like velvet. This was once a symbol of love. Now it's just another corpse.
withered-rose.pickupText: You pluck the withered rose from the dead garden. A thorn pricks your finger, drawing a single drop of blood.
withered-rose.roomText: A single withered black rose stands among the dead plants, impossibly intact.

iron-key.name: Iron Key
iron-key.description: A large iron key, crude and ancient. It's labeled 'Vault Door.' This would open the final sealed door in the cellar — the chamber where Bartholomew Blackwood's greatest sin is hidden.
iron-key.pickupText: You take the iron key from the corner. The metal is so cold it burns your fingers.
iron-key.roomText: An iron key lies in the corner near the canvas-wrapped shape, as if dropped in haste.

ritual-dagger.name: Ritual Dagger
ritual-dagger.description: A ceremonial dagger with a blade of blackened silver. The handle is carved bone — human bone, you realize with horror. This was used in the Blackwood family's dark rituals, the instrument of their damnation.
ritual-dagger.pickupText: You take the ritual dagger from the altar. Touching it makes your skin crawl, but you sense you'll need it.
ritual-dagger.roomText: A ritual dagger rests on the altar, its blade drinking what little light exists in this room.

amulet-of-binding.name: Amulet of Binding
amulet-of-binding.description: A silver amulet engraved with protective symbols — a failed attempt to control the dark forces the Blackwoods unleashed. Lady Margaret wore this the night she died. It didn't save her, but it might help you contain what waits in the cellar.
amulet-of-binding.pickupText: You take the amulet. It grows warm against your chest, as if recognizing a living soul.
amulet-of-binding.roomText: A silver amulet hangs from a hook, its chain broken as if torn off in panic.

consecrated-stake.name: Consecrated Stake
consecrated-stake.description: A wooden stake carved with prayers in Latin, blessed by a priest who knew what evil slept beneath this house. This is the only weapon that can permanently end Bartholomew Blackwood's immortal curse.
consecrated-stake.pickupText: You take the stake. It hums with holy power, warm and clean in this house of corruption.
consecrated-stake.roomText: A wooden stake leans against the wine racks, carved with Latin prayers that still hold faint luminescence.

heart-of-the-house.name: Heart of the House
heart-of-the-house.description: A pulsating crystal buried beneath the willow tree, glowing with sickly green light. This is the anchor of the house's curse — the phylactery where the Blackwood children's souls are trapped, powering the dark magic that keeps their father immortal. Destroying this would free them.
heart-of-the-house.pickupText: You dig up the Heart of the House. It pulses like a living thing, warm and nauseating in your hands. You can hear children crying inside it.
heart-of-the-house.roomText: Something glows beneath the willow tree's roots — a crystal that pulses like a beating heart.

unlock-parlor-south.description: A door to the south is secured with a silver padlock, ornately decorated with roses. It needs a matching key.
unlock-parlor-south.hintText: The silver padlock on the south door has roses engraved on it. Perhaps a key with similar decorations would fit.
unlock-parlor-south.solvedText: You insert the silver key into the padlock. It turns smoothly, and the lock falls open with a musical click. The door swings wide, revealing stairs descending to a greenhouse passage. You sense warmth and growth below — strange for such a dead house.

unlock-cellar.description: A heavy wooden door to the east has a brass padlock. It's labeled 'Wine Cellar.' The lock looks old but solid.
unlock-cellar.hintText: The cellar door has a brass padlock. A key of the same metal, labeled appropriately, would open it.
unlock-cellar.solvedText: You unlock the brass padlock with the matching key. It opens with a hollow click. The door swings inward, revealing stone steps descending into absolute darkness. Cold air rushes up, carrying the smell of earth and something worse. The cellar awaits.

reveal-hidden-study.description: The narrow door to the north is carved with occult symbols. A keyhole is hidden among the symbols, difficult to see in the dim light.
reveal-hidden-study.hintText: The journal mentions a hidden study behind the library where the rituals were performed. The symbols on the north door might be more than decoration.
reveal-hidden-study.solvedText: Reading the journal reveals the secret: the symbols aren't a lock but an illusion. You trace the pattern described in Lady Margaret's final entry, and the door clicks open on its own, revealing the hidden study where the Blackwoods damned their souls. The air beyond smells of ash and old sin.

pacify-conservatory.description: The dry fountain in the conservatory center has a small pedestal at its base. Something could be placed here as an offering.
pacify-conservatory.hintText: The ghost of a Blackwood child loved the roses in this conservatory. An offering might grant safe passage through the greenhouse.
pacify-conservatory.solvedText: You place the withered rose on the fountain pedestal. For a moment, nothing happens. Then the rose blooms — black petals unfurling, alive once more. A child's laughter echoes softly, grateful rather than sinister. The greenhouse path is safe now. One soul, at least, remembers love.

action.hazard: The vines shift and writhe, slowly encircling your ankles like patient predators.

destroy-the-heart.description: The Heart of the House pulses with malevolent energy, the phylactery binding the children's souls to this cursed place. It must be destroyed to break the curse.
destroy-the-heart.hintText: The ritual dagger that created the curse might be the only thing that can unmake it. Strike at the heart.
destroy-the-heart.solvedText: You drive the ritual dagger into the Heart of the House. The crystal screams — a sound like breaking glass and crying children. Cracks spread across its surface, light bleeding out. The house shudders. The curse is breaking. The children's souls, freed at last, manifest as gentle lights that float upward through the glass ceiling. They whisper 'thank you' as they fade. The house's grip weakens. You might actually escape this place.
destroy-the-heart.goalName: Destroy the Heart of the House

slay-the-patriarch.description: Bartholomew Blackwood's coffin rests against the far wall. The warlock who damned his family sleeps within, immortal and monstrous. Only a consecrated stake can end his unholy existence.
slay-the-patriarch.hintText: The consecrated stake was prepared by someone who knew Bartholomew Blackwood's true nature. It's blessed specifically to pierce his immortal heart.
slay-the-patriarch.solvedText: You throw open the coffin lid. Bartholomew Blackwood's eyes snap open — burning coals in a corpse's face. He rises with inhuman speed, but you're faster. The consecrated stake drives through his chest, pinning him to the coffin. He screams, a sound of rage and terror, as holy fire consumes him from within. The warlock burns, and with him, the final anchor of the curse. The house groans. The front door blows open. You're free. The nightmare is over.

action.hazard: A wet, raspy breathing echoes from the coffin. Something inside is dreaming, and dreams are stirring.
